title: Add monsters, survival bars, crafting/food system, and XP bar to Minecraft Classic 3D

requirements.text: Extend `frontend/src/hooks/useMinecraftClassic3DGame.ts` to include three classic mob types — Zombie (melee chaser), Skeleton (ranged, shoots arrows), and Spider (fast, erratic movement). Mobs spawn procedurally at a configurable distance from the player (e.g., 10–20 blocks away) and respawn over time. Each mob has HP, a simple pathfinding/chase behaviour toward the player, and an attack that reduces the player's health on contact (or when a skeleton arrow hits). Expose mob state (position, type, HP, alive) in the hook's returned state object so the 3D scene can render them.

quotes: classic mobs
monsters spawn procedurally around the world (e.g., at a certain distance from the player)

acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 mob types exist: Zombie (melee), Skeleton (ranged arrow), Spider (fast erratic).
Mobs spawn procedurally at a distance from the player position, not at the player's feet.
Each mob chases the player and deals damage on hit.
Mob state (position, type, HP, alive) is exposed from the hook for rendering.
Dead mobs are removed or hidden from the world.

requirements.text: Render the three mob types as distinct 3D meshes in `frontend/src/pages/MinecraftClassicGame.tsx` using React Three Fiber. Zombies: green box-figure; Skeletons: white/grey thin box-figure; Spiders: dark grey low-profile wider mesh. Mobs must visually move toward the player each frame driven by the hook's mob state. Skeleton arrows must also be rendered as small elongated meshes travelling toward the player.

quotes: classic mobs
Zombies (melee, close-range chasing)
Skeletons (ranged, shoot arrows)
Spiders (fast, erratic movement)

acceptanceCriteria: Each mob type has a visually distinct 3D mesh rendered in the React Three Fiber scene.
Mob meshes move in real-time based on hook state.
Skeleton arrows are rendered as travelling projectile meshes.
Mobs disappear from the scene when their HP reaches 0.

requirements.text: Add player survival stats to `frontend/src/hooks/useMinecraftClassic3DGame.ts`: (1) Health (0–20 HP) — reduced by mob attacks; when it reaches 0, the player respawns at world origin with an empty inventory. (2) Hunger (0–20) — drains at a slow constant rate over time; when empty, health decreases steadily until food is consumed. (3) Stamina (0–100) — depletes while sprinting (holding Shift) or attacking a mob; regenerates automatically when the player is idle or walking at normal speed. Expose all three values from the hook.

quotes: hunger drain over time and reduce health if empty
stamina deplete when sprinting (or attacking) and regenerate when idle
losing all health respawn the player without any supplies

acceptanceCriteria: Health bar decreases when mobs attack the player.
Reaching 0 health respawns the player at world origin with an empty inventory.
Hunger drains over time at a constant slow rate.
When hunger is at 0, health decreases over time.
Stamina depletes when sprinting (Shift held) and when attacking.
Stamina regenerates when the player is idle or walking.
All three values are exposed from the hook.

requirements.text: Add a HUD overlay to `frontend/src/pages/MinecraftClassicGame.tsx` displaying three survival bars — Health (red), Hunger (orange/yellow), and Stamina (cyan/green) — positioned at the bottom of the screen above the hotbar. Each bar should be labelled with an icon or short text (❤️ HP, 🍖 Hunger, ⚡ Stamina) and a numeric value. Style consistently with the existing retro neon arcade theme (neon glow, dark background pill). The bars update in real time from hook state.

quotes: hunger drain over time and reduce health if empty
stamina deplete when sprinting (or attacking) and regenerate when idle

acceptanceCriteria: Three bars (Health, Hunger, Stamina) are visible in the HUD overlay.
Each bar has a distinct neon color and label.
Bar widths update in real time to reflect current values.
Bars are positioned above the hotbar without obscuring gameplay.
Styling matches the existing retro neon arcade theme.

requirements.text: Add a food and crafting system to `frontend/src/hooks/useMinecraftClassic3DGame.ts`. The player starts with an empty inventory (no items, no tools). Food items (e.g., Apple, Bread, Cooked Meat) can be crafted from gathered resources (e.g., Wood + Stone → Wooden Pickaxe; Wheat/crop drops → Bread). Define a basic crafting recipe table with at least: Wooden Pickaxe (2 wood), Apple (drops randomly from leaf/wood blocks), Bread (3 wood or crop items as placeholder). Consuming food restores Hunger. Mining efficiency for stone blocks requires at least a Wooden Pickaxe — bare-hand mining of hard blocks (stone, bedrock) should be very slow or ineffective. Expose inventory contents, crafting recipes, and a `craftItem(recipe)` callback from the hook.

quotes: food items (e.g., apples, bread, cooked meat) must be found in the world or crafted from gathered materials
No starting inventory — player spawns with nothing and must mine/gather everything
Basic crafting system
Mining requires tools (can't mine stone with bare hands effectively)

acceptanceCriteria: Player spawns with zero items in inventory.
Mining wood blocks drops wood items; mining stone drops stone items.
At least 3 crafting recipes exist (e.g., Wooden Pickaxe, Bread, Apple).
Consuming food restores Hunger.
Stone and harder blocks mine very slowly or not at all without the appropriate tool.
Inventory, recipes, and craftItem callback are exposed from the hook.

requirements.text: Add a basic crafting UI panel to `frontend/src/pages/MinecraftClassicGame.tsx` that opens when the player presses `E`. The panel lists available crafting recipes, shows the required ingredients and whether the player has enough materials, and provides a 'Craft' button for each recipe. Close the panel with `E` or `Escape`. Style consistently with the retro neon arcade theme (dark modal, neon borders, glowing buttons).

quotes: Add food that I have to find and craft
Basic crafting system

acceptanceCriteria: Pressing E opens a crafting panel overlay.
All defined recipes are listed with required ingredients.
Recipes for which the player has sufficient materials show an active 'Craft' button.
Crafting consumes the required items from inventory and adds the crafted item.
Panel closes on pressing E or Escape.
Styling matches the retro neon arcade theme.

requirements.text: Add an Experience (XP) system to `frontend/src/hooks/useMinecraftClassic3DGame.ts`. The player's XP increases when: (a) a diamond block is mined (+10 XP per block), or (b) a mob is killed (+5 XP for Zombie, +7 XP for Skeleton, +6 XP for Spider). Each level requires 100 XP. When the bar fills (100 XP), the level increments and the bar resets to 0. Expose `xp`, `xpToNextLevel` (always 100), and `level` from the hook.

quotes: Add an experience bar that starts to fill up if you mine diamond or kill a mob

acceptanceCriteria: Mining a diamond block grants XP.
Killing any mob grants XP.
XP fills a 0–100 bar; reaching 100 increments the level and resets XP to 0.
xp, xpToNextLevel, and level are exposed from the hook.

requirements.text: Add an XP bar to the HUD overlay in `frontend/src/pages/MinecraftClassicGame.tsx`. Display it as a green/yellow neon progress bar labelled with '⭐ XP' and the current level (e.g., 'Level 3'). Position it alongside the Health, Hunger, and Stamina bars in the bottom HUD area. The bar fills based on current XP / xpToNextLevel and resets visually when a new level is reached. Style consistently with the existing retro neon arcade theme.

quotes: filling progressively with each XP-earning action
potentially showing your current level once the bar fills

acceptanceCriteria: XP bar is visible in the HUD overlay alongside the three survival bars.
Bar fill percentage accurately reflects current XP progress toward next level.
Current level number is displayed next to or above the bar.
Bar visually resets (animates back to 0) when levelling up.
Styling matches the retro neon arcade theme.

constraints: All changes must be confined to frontend/src/hooks/useMinecraftClassic3DGame.ts and frontend/src/pages/MinecraftClassicGame.tsx — no other game files should be modified.
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Maintain the existing pointer-lock first-person camera, WASD movement, block mining/placement, and hotbar functionality already implemented.
nonGoals: Backend persistence for inventory, XP, or survival stats.
Advanced mob AI (pathfinding around obstacles, mob spawners).
Farming or crop-growing mechanics beyond simple recipe placeholders.
Any changes to other game pages or shared layout/navigation components.
